findings.text: "It isn't one bad chain," Jaina said.
findings.reason: Fragment pivot; the surrounding explanation already carries the concrete payload and the negated setup is scaffolding.
findings.suggestedFix: "It's spread wider than that," Jaina said.

findings.text: "We're not ahead of this," Sylvanas said. "We're reading a road after the wagons passed."
findings.deletionTest: "We're reading a road after the wagons passed."
findings.reason: Preamble negation; the second sentence states the real condition and survives cleanly without the negated lead-in.
findings.suggestedFix: "We're reading a road after the wagons passed," Sylvanas said.

findings.text: It wasn't. No carts. No smoke. No voices.
findings.deletionTest: No carts. No smoke. No voices.
findings.reason: Preamble negation; the sensory inventory lands harder once the negated throat-clearing is removed.
findings.suggestedFix: No carts. No smoke. No voices.

findings.deletionTest: One house name in Lordaeron could mean rent rolls, storehouses, tenant land, old claims hanging on from a dead grandfather's bargain.
findings.reason: Fragment pivot; the following sentence already states the uncertainty, so the negated fragment is redundant scaffolding.
Fragment pivot; the next line supplies the operative conclusion, so the negated sentence is another refusal before commitment.

findings.text: Three at once was an answer already in motion.
findings.subject: answer
findings.realAgent: three coordinated strikes revealing an attack already underway
findings.reason: The sentence launders coordinated enemy action onto an abstract answer instead of naming the attack mechanics.
findings.suggestedFix: Three at once meant the attack was already underway.

findings.text: Millbrook Spur sat dead quiet when they rode in.
findings.subject: Millbrook Spur
findings.realAgent: the empty yard, dead chimney, and lack of voices
findings.reason: The place name is carrying bodily posture instead of the concrete signs that make the depot feel emptied out.
findings.suggestedFix: Millbrook Spur was dead quiet when they rode in.

findings.text: One word that struck somewhere older in memory.
findings.subject: word
findings.reason: The sentence gives the word the mental action instead of naming Sylvanas's recollection directly.
findings.suggestedFix: One word she remembered from somewhere older.

findings.text: Sylvanas kept her finger on the map and let another thought rise behind the first.
findings.subject: thought
findings.realAgent: Sylvanas turning deliberately to the old seller memory
findings.reason: The sentence externalizes Sylvanas's thinking into a rising thought instead of keeping the mental action with her.
findings.suggestedFix: Sylvanas kept her finger on the map and thought of the old grain seller next.

findings.text: Millbrook Spur had cut the choices down to one.
findings.subject: Millbrook Spur
findings.realAgent: the cleaned-depot evidence and the Barov route logic
findings.reason: The location is made to do the narrowing instead of the evidence Sylvanas has just assembled.
findings.suggestedFix: There was only one road left.

audits.cleanReason: No recurring tic breached its chapter cap. Tool-signaled lines (filter_noticed, participial_trailing, simile_like, punctuation_tics) stayed isolated rather than forming a chapter-level repetition pattern, and the lone eye beat is character-specific perimeter scanning rather than repertoire exhaustion.

summary.verdictReason: Pass A found 10 mild KILLs across negation-addiction and intent-smear; that exceeds the pass threshold of 3 or fewer mild-only kills. Rhythm and texture analytics also show structural flatness downstream editors should address.

textureMetrics.interpretation: PARTICIPIAL PHRASES: 2.1% (floor: 5.0%, human: 11.0%). Add ", Ving" phrases to connect actions. COMPOUND CLAUSES: 3.7% (floor: 4.0%, human: 8.5%). Join related ideas with ", and" / ", but" / ", yet". SHORT SENTENCES: 63.2% (ceiling: 62.0%, human: 52.2%). Too choppy; combine adjacent short sentences using the constructions above.
